Cold rain incoming, fly off to the cooler climes this weekend and you'll spend next week wrapped up in gloom.

Across Europe, the average high temperature has hit the late 30s, almost 20 degrees above average for June. The current heatwave has been unusual in that temperatures have soared in northerly areas. Yet the expectation among meteorologists is that regular patterns will resume as the summer unfolds, meaning scorching conditions in southern Europe in line with recent years, but cooler in the north. Indeed, these soaring temperatures are changing the way we travel, with Booking.com saying 54 per cent of holidaymakers are ruling out destinations deemed too hot - with a further quarter actively seeking 'cooler' trips. So maybe it's time for you to try what we might call a 'coolcation'. Here are some top tips for enjoying some warmth, without overheating, in July and August.
  • 4 comments
  • share As Europe burns in 40C sun... the best trips to ESCAPE the hot weather

  • Schools cannot win. I work in a primary school & we get criticised whatever we do. We’ve had parents complaining that we let their kids out in extreme heat and we’ve also had other parents complaining that we DON’T let kids out in the heat… We’ve been accused of not letting kids drink enough but you cannot force a child to drink if they don’t want to, because that would cause complaints as well. Parents moan about having to do the school run in the snow whilst others complain when you close due to heavy snow. We’ve had parents moaning about sending their kids home sick & others who moaned that we don’t send their kids home & it turned out they were sick. It’s honestly a joke. If you want to blame anyone for the weak, fragile, non resilience in society these days, then blame the parents who are so quick to complain about schools who are just trying to do their best.
